# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our Python Flask API. The app needs to install dependencies from requirements.txt, expose port 5000, and run the Flask application. Keep it simple and straightforward for quick deployment to production.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:3.8-slim  # or whichever version you want, e.g., "python:alpine" for alpine linux based images and so on...

# Set the working directory in the container to /app (this is where your app will be mounted)
WORKDIR /app

# Add requirements file into our image — this has been done before, but just ensure we have all dependencies installed.   # [OPTIONAL] Uncomment below line if you want a specific version of the Python application to run on Docker container: pip install -r ./requirements-dev.txt --no-cache
# RUN echo "Installing requirements..." && \  (Unnecessary as we are not installing anything, just copying files)   # [OPTIONAL] Uncomment below line if you want a specific version of the Python application to run on Docker container: pip install -r ./requirements.txt --no-cache
# Copy local code to image and set environment variable for Flask app — this is where we'll add our python script (app) that will be running in dockerized flask server, e.g., from /src/your_flask_application or wherever you have your Python application located: cp -r ./* /app
# Set environment variable for Flask app — this is where we'll add our python script (app) that will be running in dockerized flask server, e.g., from ./src/your_flask_application or wherever you have your Python application located: export FLASK_APP=./src
# Expose port for Flask app — this is where we'll add our python script (app) that will be running in dockerized flask server, e.g., from ./runserver-plus --host 0.0.0.0 -p $PORT:5000 or wherever you have your Python application located
# Expose port for Flask app and allow all traffic on the exposed ports (this is where we'll add our python script (app) that will be running in dockerized flask server, e.g., from ./runserver-plus --host 0.0.0.0 -p $PORT:5000 or wherever you have your Python application located
EXPOSE 5000   # Expose port for Flask app and allow all traffic on the exposed ports (this is where we'll add our python script (app) that will be running in dockerized flask server, e.g., from ./runserver-plus --host 0
# Run application inside Docker container with command to run your Python file: CMD [ "python", "-u" ] + ${APP_PATH} or wherever you have python script (app) located and where it should be started in the dockerized flask server, e.g., ./src/your-flask-application
# Run application inside Docker container with command to run your Python file: CMD [ "python", "-u" ] + ${APP_PATH} or wherever you have python script (app) located and where it should be started in the dockerized flask server, e.g., ./src/your-flask-application
CMD ["python3"]  # Run application inside Docker container with command to run your Python file: CMD [ "python", "-u" ] + ${APP_PATH} or wherever you have python script (app) located and where it should be started in the dockerized flask server, e.g., ./src/your-flask-application
